title: Boundary-only within-lap ranking expert using querywise CatBoost
summary: Earlier ranking ideas were broad; the sharper version is to rank only plausible pit-window candidates within each lap/query.
rationale: Earlier ranking ideas were broad; the sharper version is to rank only plausible pit-window candidates within each lap/query. CatBoost supports querywise and pairwise ranking objectives directly, which is useful because ROC AUC ultimately rewards ordering and many easy negatives need not dominate training.
implementation_hint: Build a ranker with `GroupId = Year/Race/LapNumber`. Restrict training rows or pair generation to candidate windows such as `TyreLife >= race-compound median stop age - k`, high degradation, or late-stint laps. Within each query, pair actual stoppers against candidate non-stoppers and train `YetiRankPairwise`, `QueryCrossEntropy`, or `PairLogit`; blend its OOF ranks with the classifier only on candidate windows.
expected_effect: Should improve local ordering among same-lap rivals, where the current global classifier may be strong overall but still miss which of several old-tyre cars is most likely to stop next.
risk: If candidate-window heuristics are wrong, the ranker becomes sparse/noisy; query groups with zero positives also need careful handling.
